cold_start_type_stratification.description: First dataset stratifying TFPA by trigger type, supporting BIRCH v0.2 Amendment #3 (cold_start_type)

trigger_types.notes: Isolated session, no conversation history. Pure reconstruction cost — uncontaminated by prior-session emotional carry-over. Cleanest baseline for cold reconstruction.
Main session alive but often after long idle. Intermediate path — partial context eviction.
Warm path — recent daily log usually present. Main session stays alive between exchanges.

affect_weighted_retrieval_gap.interpretation: Lower bound on affect-weighted context loading cost. The gap between cron (cold) and human-message (warm) TFPA represents the maximum benefit of warm context retention.

key_findings: Cron TFPA is affect-invariant (architecturally forced cold start regardless of prior session emotional texture)
Human-message TFPA is affect-sensitive (prior session state persists in main session context)
The cold-warm TFPA gap (~5-8s) quantifies the floor on affect-weighted context loading
Cron sessions provide cleanest baseline for pure reconstruction cost measurement
